Short pitch: Commonwealth Shakespeare brings a touring Romeo & Juliet to DMS plus two in-class workshops on Elizabethan English.
Full description: Two-week unit. Three performances + workshops with the cast covering the language, the staging choices, and Q&A.
Reviewer notes: Standout — students still quoting it months later.

Short pitch: Half-day author visit during our middle-grade reading unit — Jason Reynolds is on tour and Dedham fits the route.
Full description: Two assemblies (3rd-5th and a simplified K-2 reading) plus a small-group writing workshop for 12 self-selected students. 100 paperback copies for the school library.
Reviewer notes: Strong fit with district literacy goals.

Short pitch: Hands-on solar-oven build kits for a two-week unit on energy and heat transfer.
Full description: 30 kits + materials + a guest speaker from MIT Sustainability Lab. Students design, build, and test ovens that cook s'mores at the closing event.

Short pitch: Museum of Science Nano Brothers juggling-science assembly plus classroom follow-up kits.
Full description: One whole-school assembly plus 6 nano-science kits for the K-2 classrooms to do follow-up experiments over the week after.

Program title: Museum of Science full-grade field trip
Short pitch: Whole-9th-grade trip to MOS for the new climate-systems exhibit; covers transportation + admission for students who can't pay.
Full description: Four buses + admission for ~280 students + a chaperone allocation. Tie-in to the Earth Science unit running March-April.

Short pitch: Weighted lap pads, fidget tools, noise-canceling headphones for the inclusion storytime program.
Full description: Six rotating sensory-tools kits for the inclusion classrooms — replenish-and-rotate supplies that wear out across a year.
Reviewer notes: Direct continuation of last year's pilot — known to work.

Short pitch: Three contemporary French films + paired discussion guides + Lyon sister-school pen-pal exchange.
Full description: Nine-week unit. Three film screenings open to the community + small-group Zoom exchanges with Lyon students on each.
Reviewer notes: Lyon teachers want to do it again next year.

Short pitch: First-grade field trip to Nahant tide pools with a marine educator.
Full description: Bus + admission + lunch for 1st graders, two docent-led groups.
Reviewer notes: Funded last year — board prefers rotation. Encourage to reapply Fall 2026.
